rulings.Case Title: ANNE M SCONBERG VS. JASON WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Discharge Receiver And Exonerate Bond On Waiver Of Accounting, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support And Receivers Joinder In Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday September 15, 2016, line 1. PLAINTIFF ANNE SCONBERG Motion To Discharge Receiver And Exonerate Bond On Waiver Of Accounting, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support And Receivers Joinder In Motion IS GRANTED. Receiver is discharged and bond is exonerated. Plaintiff to pay outstanding receiver fees. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TONY BOWLES VS. KELLY HENSLEY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday September 15, 2016, line 7. DEFENDANT KELLY HENSLEY, LEFTOVERS, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is DENIED. As alleged no defect appears on the face of the complaint regarding the Statute of Limitations. i.e. "in or about June 2012" does not preclude July 2, 2015 being within the Statue of Limitations. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DEBORAH FOLLINGSTAD VS. NADIA LAMA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest The Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday September 15, 2016, line 4. DEFENDANT NADIA LAMA Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest The Good Faith Settlement is DENIED. Moving party's calculation of potential liability is primarily based on the concept of overpayment of rent. This overpayment occurred in two distinct time-frames: prior to moving party taking ownership and after the moving party took ownership. Moving party fails to make any argument or cite any law showing that she is liable based on overpayments made to the settling defendants or that the settling defendants are liable based on the overpayments made to her. In effect each cause of action alleged against all defendants is broken up into two separate causes of action alleged against each defendant based on the timing of ownership. Settling defendant satisfied their burden under City of Grand Terrace v. Sup. Ct. (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251. Moving defendant failed to show that the amount of $40,000 is not within the "ballpark" for any joint liability of Defendants. = (501/REQ)
